% Per-stage prediction and decision quality of the admission controller
% (12MP tier, normal capture; observed online). Companion to
% tab_eval_data_entry_overrun.tex.
%
% GENERATED FILE -- do not edit values by hand. Sources:
%   8U_metrics_12MP_normal_0710.xlsx
%     sheet "DecisionQuality"  -> Admitted, Admit success
%     source sheets for M/S -> skip check: for each skip,
%       compare budgetMs at the decision against the most recent observed
%       sequenceActualDurationMs of the same stage within the same session
%       (session starts #1,33,74,120,162,201,241,291,331,378,424,462).
%       Confirmed = last observed duration > budget. Est. overrun = last
%       observed duration - budget (median over all skips).
%   evaluation-table_8U_12MP_normal_SEIP-review-metrics.xlsx,
%     sheet "Admission Stage Metrics" -> UB coverage, UB slack p50,
%     duration bias/MAE, miss size p95.
% All metrics use only online-observed executions under the deployed policy.
% Full counterfactual correctness of skips (offline replay) is not claimed.
%
% Column meanings:
%   Admitted        : admit decisions / all decisions for the stage.
%   Admit success   : % of fully observed admitted stages that completed
%                     within the capture budget (M 349/350, S
%                     469/470; the complement is the single watchdog-recovered
%                     unsafe admit).
%   UB coverage     : % of evaluated executions whose observed duration
%                     stayed within the predicted upper bound.
%   UB slack p50    : median (predicted UB - observed duration).
%   Dur. bias / MAE : predicted duration minus observed duration, mean signed
%                     error and mean absolute error.
%   Miss size p95   : 95th percentile of (observed - UB) among misses.
%   Skipped         : skip decisions / all decisions.
%   Confirmed       : % of skips where the most recent observed duration of
%                     the same stage already exceeded the budget (M
%                     101/137, S 12/27) -- a model-free check.
%   Est. overrun p50: median (last observed duration - budget) over skips;
%                     negative means the last observation would have fit.

\begin{table*}[t]
  \centering
  \caption{Prediction and decision quality of the admission controller for the two optional stages (12MP tier, normal capture; all values observed online). M and S denote the anonymized multi-frame and single-frame Draft stages, respectively. Under \emph{Admit decisions}, admit success is the percentage of admitted stages, among those whose execution was fully observed (350 M-stage and 470 S-stage executions), that completed within the capture budget; the 0.3\,\%/0.2\,\% complement is one single capture whose admitted stages overran and were recovered by the watchdog, so no capture timed out. Under \emph{Upper-bound prediction}, coverage is the percentage of evaluated executions whose observed duration stayed within the predicted upper bound, slack p50 is the median headroom of that bound (predicted minus observed; smaller is tighter), bias and MAE compare predicted against observed durations (observed means 830\,ms for M and 474\,ms for S), and miss size p95 is the 95th percentile of the overshoot among bound misses, all absorbed by the watchdog margin. Under \emph{Skip decisions}, each skip is checked against the most recent observed duration of the same stage within the same burst---a model-free estimate that uses only past observations, avoiding circular justification by our own predictor: confirmed means that duration already exceeded the budget at the skip decision, and est.\ overrun p50 is the median of (last observed duration minus budget). The unconfirmed skips are near-boundary cases (S median $-8$\,ms): the controller decides against a variance-aware upper bound rather than a single past sample, trading a skipped optional stage against the strictly worse capture timeout. Counterfactual correctness of skips beyond this check would require offline replay and is not claimed.}
  \label{tab:eval_pred_quality}
  \resizebox{\textwidth}{!}{%
  \begin{tabular}{l||c|c||c|r|r|r|r||c|c|r}
  \toprule \midrule
  \multirow{2}{*}{\textbf{Stage}} &
  \multicolumn{2}{c||}{\textbf{Admit decisions}} &
  \multicolumn{5}{c||}{\textbf{Upper-bound prediction}} &
  \multicolumn{3}{c}{\textbf{Skip decisions (vs.\ last observation)}} \\
  \cmidrule(lr){2-3}\cmidrule(lr){4-8}\cmidrule(l){9-11}
  & \textbf{Admitted} & \makecell{\textbf{Success}\\{\scriptsize \%}}
  & \makecell{\textbf{Coverage}\\{\scriptsize \%}} & \makecell{\textbf{Slack p50}\\{\scriptsize ms}} & \makecell{\textbf{Bias}\\{\scriptsize ms}} & \makecell{\textbf{MAE}\\{\scriptsize ms}} & \makecell{\textbf{Miss p95}\\{\scriptsize ms}}
  & \textbf{Skipped} & \makecell{\textbf{Confirmed}\\{\scriptsize \%}} & \makecell{\textbf{Est.\ overrun p50}\\{\scriptsize ms}} \\
  \midrule \midrule
  M & 366/503 & 99.7 & 91.1 & 145 & $+0.9$ & 64 & 228 & 137/503 & 73.7 & $+148$ \\
  S & 476/503 & 99.8 & 94.0 &  60 & $-3.4$ & 26 &  96 &  27/503 & 44.4 & $-8$   \\
  \midrule \bottomrule
  \end{tabular}%
  }
\end{table*}
